sales.title:
Constantius I, as Caesar (AD 305-306). BI follis or nummus (29mm, 12.11 gm, 11h). NGC MS 5/5 - 5/5, Silvering. Ticinum, 1st officina, AD 296-297. CONSTANTIVS NOB CAES, laureate head of Constantius I right / GENIO POPV-LI ROMANI, Genius standing facing, polos on head left, nude but for chlamys across left shoulder, patera in right hand, cornucopia in left; star in left field, PT in exergue. RIC VI 32a. Brilliantly lustrous and defined. Constantius I, also known as Constantius Chlorus, had a brief but intriguing reign as Roman Emperor from AD 305 to 306. His rule was marked by both notable achievements and challenges. Constantius played a pivotal role in the Tetrarchy, a unique system of joint rule, managing the Western Roman Empire with a focus on economic stability, frontier defense, and the persecution of Christians. He showcased military prowess, portrayed here on this bronze issue, winning battles against the Allemanni and securing Roman control in Britannia. Nonetheless, challenges persisted, notably the usurpation of Britannia by Carausius, a rival emperor. Constantius's reign, captured in primary sources like Eumenius's panegyrics and contemporary inscriptions, lays the foundation for the later ascent of his son, Constantine the Great, and the transformative events that reshaped the Roman Empire. HID05401242017 © 2025 Heritage Auctions | All Rights Reserved www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice

RIC VI 16. Well-centered, crisp dies with ebony toning. Ex Freeman & Sear, Mail Bid Sale 14 (21 June 2007), lot 481. Nonetheless, challenges persisted, notably the usurpation of Britannia by Carausius, a rival emperor. RIC VI 187a. Wonderfully detailed portrait. Ex Freeman & Sear, Mail Bid Sale 14 (21 June 2007), lot 492.
